lesson.menu_desc: Solve an issue on a call the professional way -- apologize, reassure, and offer a solution -- with the first conditional (If we ship today, it will arrive Monday) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- Handling Problems & Complaints -- solving an issue on a call with the first conditional and modal verbs
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Andreia Heins | Aula 17 -- Handling Problems & Complaints

listenings.text: Hi Andreia, David Lewis here. I am sorry to call with a problem. The supplier just told me the new console will be delayed by two weeks, and the training team is not happy. One of the surgeons already filed a complaint, because we promised the equipment for this month. I know this is urgent, so here is what I need. Please call the supplier and find out the real reason for the delay. If the part is missing, ask when it will ship. If they cannot give us a firm date, we will ask for a replacement unit on loan. Above all, apologize to the team and reassure them that we have a backup plan. Offer a clear solution and a new date. If you handle this calmly, the team will trust us again. Thank you, Andreia. Call me after you speak to them.

Good afternoon, everyone. I want to update you on the console, and first, I apologize for the delay and the inconvenience. Let me explain the problem and the solution. The supplier had a missing part, so the unit is delayed by two weeks. I know this is urgent for the training team. Here is the plan. If the new date slips again, we will use a loan unit as a backup, so practice will not stop. I have asked the supplier for a written commitment. If they confirm by Friday, we will keep the original schedule. I want to reassure you that no real surgery is affected. If anyone is still worried, please come and talk to me. We will fix this together, and I will send a short update every week. Thank you for your patience.

extra_audio.text: Let me describe how Andreia handles the complaint call. First, she apologizes for the delay and the inconvenience. Then she explains the problem: the supplier had a missing part. Next, she offers a solution with the first conditional: if the date slips again, we will use a backup unit. After that, she reassures the team that no real surgery is affected. Finally, she promises a clear new date and a weekly update.
